outcomes.title: Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents
outcomes.description: A software framework integrating symbolic planning methods (hierarchical task networks, MCTS) with LLM-based reasoning, enabling agents to decompose complex goals into verifiable sub-task graphs with formal pre/post-conditions.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Open-source release; integration into AI-on-demand platform; licensing for commercial adoption

outcomes.title: Adaptive tri-store memory architecture
outcomes.description: A unified memory system combining working, episodic, and semantic memory stores with a learned controller, enabling AI agents to maintain coherence over extended interaction sequences without retraining.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Open-source library; research publications; integration with commercial LLM platforms

outcomes.title: Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
outcomes.description: A set of task-delegation, negotiation, and conflict-resolution protocols for heterogeneous AI agents with mathematically proven convergence guarantees and full auditability.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Protocol standardisation proposal; open-source reference implementation; research publications

outcomes.title: Clinical decision support agent system (TRL 5)
outcomes.description: A validated AI agent system for clinical diagnostic reasoning, demonstrated on 500 de-identified clinical cases, integrating multi-source patient data with specialist sub-agent coordination.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Clinical validation pathway; licensing to health IT vendors; EDIH-mediated dissemination to hospitals

outcomes.title: Manufacturing process optimisation agent system (TRL 5)
outcomes.description: A validated multi-agent system for real-time manufacturing process monitoring, diagnosis, and optimisation, demonstrated in operational factory environments.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Licensing to manufacturing systems integrators; EIT Manufacturing network; factory pilot scaling

outcomes.title: Logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator (TRL 5)
outcomes.description: A validated agent-based logistics planning system for multi-actor supply chain coordination with disruption recovery, demonstrated on real operational scenarios. Serves as the cross-sector transfer demonstrator confirming the MAESTRO architecture's portability beyond the call's specifically identified Apply AI sectors.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Integration into ELI operations; licensing to logistics technology providers; EDIH dissemination; cross-sector transferability evidence for automotive and other domains

outcomes.title: External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)
outcomes.description: A production-grade cross-cutting tool orchestration layer providing typed tool registry, formal tool selection policies, standardised API adapters, post-invocation verification, execution monitoring with fallback/recovery, and auditable tool traces. Integrated with the planning, memory, and coordination pillars.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Bundled with open-source MAESTRO framework; standalone library for tool-augmented agent development; AI-on-demand platform contribution

outcomes.title: MAESTRO agent evaluation benchmark suite
outcomes.description: A comprehensive benchmark suite for evaluating AI agent capabilities across planning, memory, coordination, and domain-specific task completion, with standardised metrics and reproducible evaluation protocols.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Public benchmark hosted on AI-on-demand platform; adoption as community standard; research publications

outcomes.title: Open-source MAESTRO framework
outcomes.description: A production-grade open-source framework for building next-generation AI agents with pluggable planning, memory, and coordination modules, enabling European researchers and SMEs to build on MAESTRO's advances.
outcomes.exploitation_route: Open-source community development; AI-on-demand platform; SME adoption through EDIH network
